channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: This Columnist Predicted in 2006 That the Iran War Would End by 2026 (Seriously)
item.description: She also predicted Dick Cheney would become president and most drugs would be legal in 2026.

item.title: Ces 45 planètes sont désormais les cibles prioritaires pour traquer la vie autour de nous !
item.description: Si vous deviez partir à la rencontre d’une civilisation extraterrestre, par quelle planète commenceriez-vous ? Pour ceux d’entre vous qui seraient en manque d’inspiration, des chercheurs publient aujourd’hui une sorte de guide touristique : les 45 exoplanètes qui ont le plus de chance d’abriter...

item.title: John Stamos debated live-streaming his first tattoo at SXSW: Is the future of media ‘life in real-time’?
item.description: Speaking with Fast Company at SXSW, the ‘Full House’ actor shared why he’s excited to be the chief innovation officer of streaming platform Zeam. Actor John Stamos is thinking a lot more about potential opportunities to live-stream these days—be it at New York’s Thanksgiving Day parade, performing with the Beach Boys when he heads to Route 66 for the 100-year anniversary later this year, or even while getting his first-ever tattoo in Austin for SXSW. ...

item.title: On a conduit le Toyota BZ5 électrique en Chine et le constat est rude malgré une technologie BYD
item.description: Nous avons pu conduire la nouvelle voiture électrique de Toyota directement en Chine : le crossover BZ5. Voici notre premier avis.

item.title: Oeuf is a punishing platformer in a cozy shell
item.description: The funny shape of eggs is the curious lifeblood of Oeuf, the new physics platformer by prolific developer Increpare Games. In a gaming landscape saturated with complex systems dropped into simple games, that grapples with metaphor within straightforward narratives, and that is desperate to bring cinematic sensibilities into gaming, Oeuf only asks that you briefly […]...

item.title: Why do gamers already hate DLSS 5? Here are 3 key reasons — and why history suggests Nvidia will win them over eventually
item.description: Remember the launch of the very first take on DLSS? Remember when frame generation initially arrived? They weren't at all well-received, but Nvidia turned that around.

item.title: How companies and nonprofits are tackling the U.S. healthcare crisis—until there’s a federal policy solution
item.description: Speaking with Fast Company at SXSW, healthcare leaders said their solutions are only Band-Aid fixes for a system that is very much broken. The healthcare crisis in the U.S. is one marked by rising costs, coverage gaps, staggering medical debt, and attacks on access.

item.title: Dreame’s self-cleaning L10s Pro Ultra is nearly $1,000 off its original list price
item.description: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale doesn’t kick off until next week, but the retailer is already dropping a host of good deals, especially on robot vacuums. We recently saw Ecovacs’ Deebot X8 and X9 Pro Omni drop to $599 ($501 off) and $679 ($621 off), respectively; however, if you’re looking for a cheaper robot vacuum / […]

item.title: 'I've tasted blood, I've worn it' — how The Pitt season 2 prepped Shawn Hatosy for the guts and gore of Ready or Not 2: Here I Come
item.description: We're treated to double helpings of Shawn Hatosy this week in The Pitt season 2 and Ready or Not 2, and his time in the ER was the weirdest kind of preparation possible.

item.title: Chez Darty, le Xiaomi 15T (Amoled, 120 Hz) s’accompagne des Redmi Buds 6 dans un pack vendu avec une réduction de presque 30 %
item.description: Avec ce pack smartphone et écouteurs de la marque Xiaomi, vous aurez entre les mains deux appareils qui brillent grâce à leur autonomie pour à peine plus de 500 euros.

item.title: 71 Best Podcasts (2026): True Crime, Culture, Science, Fiction
item.description: Get your fix of tech, true crime, pop culture, or comedy with these audio adventures.

item.title: The new MacBook Pro is still fast as hell
item.description: Apple's flagship 16-inch MacBook Pro has reigned supreme in the world of creator-focused laptops since its M-series processor overhaul in 2021. Since then, we've mostly seen the same design with year-over-year chip bumps and small refinements. "If it ain't broke," right? If you want to know everything about this machine, you can read our review […]

item.title: An early contender for movie of the year
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 120, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, get ready for Pen Opinions, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) This week, I've been reading about Banksy and music apps and influencer agents, watching […]

item.title: Engadget review recap: Lots of Apple devices, Galaxy S26, Dell XPS 16 and more
item.description:
Apple already announced a lot of new devices in 2026 and we’ve been busy reviewing them all. In this installment of our bi-weekly roundup, we revisit the MacBook Neo, iPhone 17e and more, in addition to the “regular” Galaxy S26 and Dell XPS 16. There’s even more than those gadget to catch up on, so sit back, relax and cozy up to some fresh reviews. Apple MacBook Neo
The main attraction for Apple’s early device deluge was the $599 MacBook Neo. The company is finally giving us something we’ve been begging for: a low-cost Mac laptop that’s good enough for most people. “It's a $599 computer tha...
